title: Direct On-Wafer Measurements of Noise Parameters in C- and X-bands at $T=4$ K
authors: Daniil Frolov, Jean-Olivier Plouchart, Utku Soylu
abstract: This paper describes the setup and the results of the direct on-wafer measurements of a FET noise parameters obtained with a source-pull method at temperatures down to T=4K and in the 5-12 GHz frequency range. The setup consists of a cryostat with wafer probes, two reflectometers, a programmable impedance generator, wideband isolators and bias tees and low noise preamplifier, all cooled to cryogenic temperatures, allowing to perform a full vector error-corrected wafer-level measurements of the discrete transistors and amplifier dies. The setup and its calibration procedure are designed in a such way that allows simultaneous calibration, S-parameters, noise parameters and I-V curve measurements of several FETs all in one cooldown. Using the described setup we perform first measurements of 14nm FinFETs and also measure noise parameters of an LNA based on these FETs. Resulting noise temperature values are compared against those obtained using independent and alternative measurement techniques.
summary: This paper presents a new method for measuring the noise properties of semiconductor transistors directly on a wafer at 4 K. By using a cryogenic preamplifier and a specialized calibration technique, the researchers can measure low-gain devices that would otherwise be hidden by room-temperature noise. This is crucial for developing the readout electronics needed for quantum computing and radio astronomy.
main_problem: The difficulty of performing accurate, direct on-wafer noise parameter measurements of low-gain transistors at cryogenic temperatures (4 K) because their output noise power is often below the room-temperature instrumentation noise floor.
main_result: The development of a vector-corrected measurement framework that enables simultaneous S-parameter and noise parameter extraction for 14nm FinFETs and LNAs at 4 K, successfully overcoming the room-temperature noise floor using a cryogenic preamplifier.
method: A combination of the 'source-pull' and 'cold-source' methods using a four-state cryogenic tuner, integrated with a TRL (thru-reflect-line) calibration scheme and a cryogenic preamplification chain.
model_or_system: A cryogenic measurement platform consisting of a 4 K cryostat with wafer probes, a four-port VNA, a cryogenic impedance generator (tuner), and a preamplification chain, used to characterize 14nm FinFETs and LNA dies.
key_observables: S-parameters (S11, S21, S12, S22), noise parameters (minimum noise temperature Te, minimum noise factor Fmin, optimal reflection coefficient Gamma_opt, and Lange invariant 4N), and I-V curves.
important_parameters: Temperature of 4 K; frequency range of 5-12 GHz (C- and X-bands); 14nm FinFET technology.
main_assumptions: Assumes S21 = S12 reciprocity for the probe; assumes high circulator isolation and good matching to simplify the mismatch coefficient.
figures_summary: Schematics of the measurement stand and electrical signal paths; physical layout of cryogenic hardware; signal flow graphs and calibration planes; and plots of FET/LNA S-parameters, noise temperatures, and optimal impedances.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the scientific challenge, describes the hardware architecture and signal path, details the operational modes (S-parameter and noise parameter), explains the TRL calibration and de-embedding procedure, presents the characterization of the FETs and LNAs, and concludes with validation and future improvements.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Fig. 1. Noise parameter measurement stand consists of microscope 1, cryostat 2, control computer 3, vector network analyzer (VNA) 4, temperature readout 5, source-measure units (SMU) 6 for DC-biasing.

Fig. 2. Electrical block-diagram of the measurement instruments and hard- ware. Two major components are the VNA (Keysight 5242B) and the cryo wafer prober (Form Factor), in between there are minor room temperature components amplifier 22 (Mini-Circuits) and directional coupler 32. The VNA equivalent circuit includes: RF source 1; terminated switches 2,10,11,18; SPDT switch 20; heterodyne receivers R1...R4, A,B,C,D; noise receiver 21; and directional couplers 3,6,9,14,17,19. The RF hardware inside the cryo- stat includes: 4-12 GHz triple junction ferrite isolator 23, 3.4-12.3 GHz circulator 28, 0.3-14 GHz low noise amplifier (Low noise factory) 30, 30 dB directional coupler 24, 0.2-18 GHz...

Fig. 3. Cryogenic instrumentation mounted on the 4K plate of the refrigerator: front view and top view (as seen by the microscope), with the same numbering as in block diagram in fig.2. Probes P1, P2, P3 are moved by Attocube piezo- based drives for cryogenic environments.
Fig. 4. Signal graph of the system representing DUT, error boxes and five calibration planes required for calibration of 24 error terms.
Fig. 5. Characterization of the pre-amplifier. (a) preamp is connected to the tuner, (b) preamp is connected to the P1 for S-parameter measurement
